ticket.summary: Airbyte sync stuck for BCBSMA — CDC connector fails at 14:23 UTC

symptom.description: Scheduled Airflow DAG sync-bcbsma failed on its 14:23 UTC run. The Airbyte connector for BCBSMA source raised a ConnectionResetError during CDC extraction and did not retry successfully. Data has not been landed to Snowflake since 12:00 UTC.
symptom.error_quote:
ERROR airbyte.workers.temporal — ConnectionResetError:
[Errno 104] Connection reset by peer
  at BCBSMA Postgres CDC · offset LSN 0/1A2B3C4D

root_cause.description: The saved CDC offset on the BCBSMA connector references a WAL position that the source Postgres has already recycled. Airbyte cannot resume from that LSN, so every replication attempt fails with a connection reset when the WAL segment is requested and immediately closed by Postgres.

reference.title: Same connector, same root cause · resolved 2026-03-15

steps.subtitle: From Confluence runbook: Resolving CDC Offset Error

items.title: Open the Airbyte UI and locate the BCBSMA connection
items.detail: Airbyte → Connections → BCBSMA → Snowflake CURATED. Confirm status shows Failed with the most recent run at 14:23 UTC.

items.title: Reset the saved CDC offset
items.detail: Click Clear data on the connection. This drops the stored LSN so the next sync starts a fresh snapshot from the source.

items.title: Trigger a manual sync and confirm the run starts cleanly
items.detail: Click Sync now. First run will do a full snapshot (~35 min for BCBSMA); subsequent runs will resume incremental CDC.

items.title: Notify BCBSMA data-ops that a re-snapshot is in progress
items.detail: Post in #client-bcbsma-notifications with expected recovery window. Any downstream Snowflake tables will be stale until the first sync completes.

verify: First sync completes green — Airflow DAG sync-bcbsma shows success in the next scheduled run.
Monitor two full sync cycles (~1h) on the Datadog · Airbyte / BCBSMA dashboard with no ConnectionResetError.
Row counts in PROD_CURATED.BCBSMA.* match source-side counts within tolerance (± 0.5%).
Confirm no PHI/PII was exposed in any log line during the incident (grep-scan attached log for MRN/SSN patterns).
